beats.narration_text: Hallo. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of spin-½ uncertainty ensemble. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of spin-½ uncertainty ensemble

beats.narration_text: Spin-½ Uncertainty Ensemble is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Robertson bound σ. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Concept card: Spin-½ Uncertainty Ensemble

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Spin-½ Uncertainty Ensemble. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: thetaSlider, phiSlider. Verify: Robertson bound σ. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Spin-½ Uncertainty Ensemble. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: thetaSlider, phiSlider. Verify: Robertson b

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from 04-uncertainty-ensemble.html

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Show the core physics loop from 04-uncertainty-ensemble.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: spin-½ uncertainty ensemble. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag main slider from 0 to 180. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of main slider from 0 to 180

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Slate: narrate slider sweep of main slider from 0 to 180

beats.narration_text: Robertson bound σ. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of spin-½ uncertainty ensemble. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Spin-½ Uncertainty Ensemble simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.

props.command: Take this Spin-½ Uncertainty Ensemble simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Outro: Spin-½ Uncertainty Ensemble · @Medhavy
